title: helianthemum crassifolium subsp. sphaerocalyx
intro: Helianthemum crassifolium subsp. sphaerocalyx, also known as Rockrose, is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, pink to white flowers and narrow leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Helianthemum crassifolium subsp. sphaerocalyx include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce fever and inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and creams for topical application.
